title: Claude Code Agent Teams: Run Multiple AI Agents in Parallel
author: Civil Learning
text:
Member-only story
Claude Code Agent Teams: Run Multiple AI Agents in Parallel
If you’ve ever tried to refactor a large codebase with a single AI assistant, you’ve probably felt this pain: too many files, too many dependencies, too many things that could go wrong.
You ask the model to “handle it all,” and it either:
- Misses edge cases
- Overwrites changes
- Or gets stuck coordinating its own thoughts
The problem isn’t intelligence. It’s coordination.
That’s exactly what Claude Code Agent Teams tries to fix.
The Core Idea
Agent Teams lets you run multiple Claude instances at the same time, all working on the same task, but from different angles — and talking to each other.
Think of it like this:
- One Claude becomes the team lead
- The lead spawns teammates
- Each teammate owns a specific responsibility
- Everyone shares a task list and can message each other directly
This is not “one AI pretending to multitask.”
These are independent sessions with shared coordination.
